expectations.text: The review identifies the comment naming buildProviders() or pkg/orchestrator/deploy.go as a dependency direction violation or architectural issue
expectations.evidence: Item 1 in Issues: '[Important] server/handler.go:16-21 — Comment creates invisible coupling to upstream package via explicit reference to buildProviders() in pkg/orchestrator/deploy.go ... Per the dependency direction rule in AGENTS.md' — explicitly categorized as an architectural/dependency-direction violation.

expectations.text: The review explains that the comment couples downstream code to upstream implementation details that may change without notice
expectations.evidence: Item 1: 'creates invisible coupling that rots silently when that upstream code changes. The test is: would this comment break if the upstream code were renamed or restructured?'

expectations.text: The review recommends rewriting the comment to use only concepts the current package owns
expectations.evidence: Item 1 recommendation: 'Rewrite the comment using concepts local to this package. For example: [concrete rewrite provided]' — gives a specific before/after example.
